title: Check the big people-search brokers
why: People-search sites assemble name history, addresses, relatives, and phone numbers — and they often show former names. This is where a deadname is most likely to be sitting in plain sight, and it's the exposure you can most directly remove later.
instructions: Open each broker's own search page (links below) and look yourself up.
Search your current name, and — when you're ready — your former name.
For anything that shows your former name or ties it to your current one, add it to your ledger and mark whether it exposes your deadname.
Don't submit an opt-out yet — that's Phase 2. Right now you're just mapping what's out there.
deadnamePrompts: Does the listing show a 'previously known as' / AKA / name-history field?
Are relatives or old addresses listed that would help someone connect your names?
